course_description:
Research project. Students completing the course in a single semester must enrol in MRES7018. Students completing the course over 2 semesters must enrol in MRES7019 (start semester 1, finish semester 2) or MRES7020 (start semester 2, finish semester 1).

From 2025 - To enrol in MRES7019 externally, students require independent access to an MRI scanner. If this is not possible, students will need to be on site at the Centre of Advanced Imaging for approximately a 2-3 week period.

This course is offered as part of the Master ofﾠMagnetic Resonance Technology.

A two week residential component at St Lucia campus is required for this course. The timetabling of this will be dependent upon the individual student’s project and will be decided in conjunction with the student’s supervisor early in the semester.

This is a 6 unit research project offered by the Centre, reflecting the importance of the residential component offering practical experience in the area of imaging.

Prospective students are required to consult with the course coordinator at least 6 week prior to course commencement date as the project availability is limited.

Additional timetable information
A two week residential component at St Lucia campus is required for this course. The timetabling of this will be dependent upon the individual studentﾒs project and will be decided in conjunction with the studentﾒs supervisor early in the semester.
course_aims: Conduct a research project using imaging.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply theory gained in MRES7001 & 7004 to conduct a research project using imaging.
Collect, analyse and organise information and convey that information clearly and fluently.
Analyse results and discuss them in relation to imaging.

assessment_details.title: Project Definition
assessment_details.task_description: Project proposal must include aim(s), justification, method(s), equipment and bibliography. Students should contact the course coordinator to discuss the project proposal and related ethical considerations. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for extension/deferral information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item then the listed penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: PowerPoint Slides and Oral Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: You will need to prepare a series of PowerPoint slides which you will be required to present orally to an audience of students and staff. Your oral presentation should outline the aim(s) of the project, existing knowledge, the approach you have taken, results, conclusions and what remains to be done. You will need to explain your technical achievements and put them into context. It is mandatory to attend the presentation session and contribute to audience questions to other students’ presentations. Failure to present your work orally will results in a fail grade regardless of whether you have submitted your presentation to Turniitin. Read full task description Hide full task description
